questions.text: What is the correct relationship between data and information in the context of information security?
options: A. Data is structured and processed information
B. Information is the meaning and value assigned to a set of data
C. Data and information are interchangeable and equivalent terms
D. Information is the raw and unprocessed form of data
questions.justification_correct: Information results from assigning meaning, context, and value to raw data — a fundamental distinction in ISO 27000:2018, which defines information as data with relevance and purpose. (ISO/IEC 27000:2018, Clause 3.9)
questions.justification_wrong: Data is the raw form; information is data with assigned meaning. The terms are not interchangeable in the standard's terminology.

questions.text: To determine the value of organizational data for insurance contract purposes, which factor is NOT relevant?
options: A. The intrinsic content of the data
B. The ease of recovery in case of loss, incompleteness, or inaccuracy
C. The color or visual format used in presenting the data
D. The importance of business processes that utilize the data
questions.justification_correct: Color and visual format are aesthetic attributes with no impact on the strategic, operational, or compliance value of data for the organization. Value is determined by content, recoverability, and criticality to business processes.
questions.justification_wrong: Content, recoverability, and criticality to processes are factors directly relevant to determining the value of organizational data.

questions.text: A hacker gains access to a server and manages to view a file containing credit card numbers. Which principle of the CIA triangle was violated?
options: A. Availability
B. Confidentiality
C. Integrity
D. Authenticity
questions.justification_correct: Confidentiality was violated — data was accessed by an unauthorized entity, exposing sensitive information to third parties without permission. (ISO/IEC 27000:2018, Clause 3.10)
questions.justification_wrong: Availability refers to legitimate access; integrity to absence of unauthorized modifications; unauthorized access without modification exclusively violates confidentiality.

questions.text: Printed documents are frequently left in the network printer in the hallway. What consequence does this have for information confidentiality?
options: A. The integrity of the information is no longer guaranteed
B. The availability of the information is no longer guaranteed
C. The confidentiality of the information is no longer guaranteed
D. The authenticity of the information is no longer guaranteed
questions.justification_correct: Documents left in accessible locations violate confidentiality, as any person in the hallway can access information that should be restricted. (ISO/IEC 27002:2022, Clause 5.15)
questions.justification_wrong: Integrity would be affected by alterations; availability by inability to access; the issue here is uncontrolled physical access to information.

questions.text: A well-conducted risk analysis has four main objectives. Which one is NOT among them?
options: A. Identify assets and their values
B. Directly implement countermeasures during the analysis phase
C. Establish balance between incident costs and security measure costs
D. Determine relevant vulnerabilities and threats
questions.justification_correct: Risk analysis identifies, assesses, and prioritizes risks — the implementation of countermeasures is a subsequent step in risk treatment, not an objective of the analysis itself. (ISO/IEC 27005:2022, Clause 5.3)
questions.justification_wrong: Identifying assets, establishing cost-benefit balance, and determining vulnerabilities are legitimate objectives of risk analysis according to ISO 27005.

questions.text: What is the term for a possible event that can have a detrimental effect on information confidentiality?
options: A. Dependency
B. Threat
C. Vulnerability
D. Risk
questions.justification_correct: A threat is defined as a potential cause of an unwanted incident that can result in harm to a system or organization — as per ISO 27000:2018, Clause 3.74. (ISO/IEC 27000:2018)
questions.justification_wrong: Vulnerability is a weakness that can be exploited; risk is the combination of probability and impact; dependency is a relationship between components — none of these is correct.

questions.text: What is the central purpose of risk management in information security?
options: A. To determine with precision the statistical probability of each risk occurring
B. To calculate the exact value of damage caused by security incidents
C. To map all threats to which IT resources are exposed
D. To implement measures to reduce risks to a level acceptable to the organization
questions.justification_correct: Risk management aims to treat identified risks through controls that reduce them to a level acceptable according to the organization's risk appetite. (ISO/IEC 27005:2022, Clause 5)
questions.justification_wrong: Probability, exact damage calculation, and threat mapping are activities of risk assessment, not the central purpose of risk management.

questions.text: What characterizes a qualitative risk analysis?
options: A. It follows precise statistical probability calculations to determine exact losses
B. It is based on scenarios and situations, producing a subjective view of possibilities
C. It uses exclusively quantified historical data from previous incidents
D. It requires automated tools for processing large volumes of data
questions.justification_correct: Qualitative risk analysis is based on judgment, scenarios, and descriptive scales (high/medium/low) rather than precise numerical values — a common approach when statistical data is insufficient.
questions.justification_wrong: Precise calculation and quantified historical data characterize quantitative analysis; automated tools are auxiliaries, not defining characteristics of the approach.

questions.text: After a fire at an insurance company's branch, the server was destroyed and the backup tapes melted. Which is an example of INDIRECT damage caused by the fire?
options: A. Backup tapes melted by heat
B. Computer systems burned directly
C. Documents consumed by fire
D. Damage caused by water from fire extinguishers
questions.justification_correct: Damage caused by water from fire extinguishers is an indirect consequence of fighting the fire, not from the fire itself — exemplifying collateral damage. Tapes, servers, and documents are direct fire damages.
questions.justification_wrong: Tapes, servers, and documents are direct damages caused by heat and fire — they are not indirect consequences of the event.

questions.text: A company decides to implement measures only for large risks, accepting small ones without action. What is this risk strategy called?
options: A. Risk acceptance
B. Risk avoidance
C. Risk neutralization
D. Risk transfer
questions.justification_correct: Risk acceptance is the conscious decision to take no action on certain risks, usually those with lower impact or probability, in accordance with the organization's risk appetite. (ISO/IEC 27005:2022)
questions.justification_wrong: Risk avoidance eliminates the activity generating the risk; risk mitigation implements controls; risk transfer transfers the risk to third parties — none of these describes conscious acceptance.

questions.text: Which of the following is an example of a human-origin threat?
options: A. Infected USB drive introduced into the network by an employee
B. Excessive dust accumulation in the server room
C. Voltage variation causing partial failure in power supply
D. Hardware failure due to natural wear of components
questions.justification_correct: The introduction of an infected USB drive is human action — intentional or unintentional — that constitutes a human-origin threat. Dust, voltage variation, and wear are threats of environmental or technical origin. (ISO/IEC 27001:2022)
questions.justification_wrong: Dust, electrical variations, and hardware wear are threats of non-human origin — environmental or technical.

questions.text: An organization is establishing its Information Security Management System (ISMS) in accordance with ISO 27001:2022. During the planning phase, senior management needs to define how it will communicate its commitment to information security to employees and interested parties. Which document formally established by the standard is responsible for expressing this strategic direction?
options: A. Information Security Objectives, which must be measurable and aligned with business processes
B. Information Security Policy, which must be approved by senior management and communicated appropriately
C. Risk Treatment Plan, which details the corrective and preventive actions for each identified risk
D. Responsibility Matrix (RACI), which defines the roles of each department in ISMS implementation
questions.justification_correct: In accordance with ISO 27001:2022, clause 5.2 (Policy), the organization must establish an information security policy that is approved by senior management and communicated appropriately within and outside the organization.
questions.justification_wrong: Option A (Objectives) defines specific targets, not overall strategic direction; Option C (Treatment Plan) is operational, focused on specific risks; Option D (RACI Matrix) defines roles and responsibilities.

questions.text: An employee receives a call from someone claiming to be from the call center requesting their password. What type of threat is this?
options: A. Natural-origin threat
B. Organizational-origin threat
C. Social engineering
D. Technical-origin threat
questions.justification_correct: Social engineering manipulates people into disclosing confidential information or performing actions that compromise security — in this case, via vishing (voice phishing). (ISO/IEC 27002:2022, Control 6.3)
questions.justification_wrong: Natural and technical threats do not involve human manipulation; organizational threats refer to process failures — psychological manipulation characterizes social engineering.

questions.text: An organization has detected a security incident involving unauthorized access to customer data. According to ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Clause 6.1.3 and ISO/IEC 27002:2022 control 5.24, which activity is MOST critical during the incident response phase to support continual improvement of the ISMS?
options: A. Immediately notifying all affected customers before completing the incident investigation
B. Collecting and preserving evidence while documenting the incident timeline, actions taken, and lessons learned
C. Restoring systems to operational status as quickly as possible without documentation
D. Escalating the incident to executive management before assessing its severity and impact
questions.justification_correct: Option B is correct. ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Clause 6.1.3 requires organizations to retain documented information on information security risks, and control 5.24 (Information security incident management planning and preparation) in ISO/IEC 27002:2022 emphasizes the importance of evidence collection, documentation of incident handling procedures, and learning from incidents. Proper documentation including evidence preservation, incident timeline, response actions, and lessons learned is essential for: (1) supporting potential legal or regulatory requirements, (2) enabling post-incident analysis and review as required by Clause 10.1 (Nonconformity and corrective action), (3) facilitating continual improvement of the ISMS through corrective actions, and (4) updating risk assessments and treatment plans. This documentation directly supports the Plan-Do-Check-Act cycle fundamental to ISMS implementation.
questions.justification_wrong: Option A is incorrect because premature customer notification before completing investigation may provide inaccurate information and does not address ISMS improvement requirements; notification timing should be based on legal/regulatory obligations and factual assessment. Option C is incorrect as restoring systems without documentation violates ISO 27001:2022 requirements for retaining documented information and prevents learning from incidents, which is essential for continual improvement under Clause 10. Option D is incorrect because escalation should follow defined incident classification and severity assessment procedures as specified in the incident management plan; premature escalation before proper assessment wastes management resources and may trigger inappropriate responses.

questions.text: Among examples of threats to information reliability, which specifically represents a threat to integrity?
options: A. Loose cable causing connection interruption
B. Accidental alteration of data by an employee
C. Unauthorized personal use of organizational data
D. Identity falsification to gain system access
questions.justification_correct: Accidental alteration of data compromises integrity — the property that information has not been modified in an unauthorized or unintentional manner. (ISO/IEC 27000:2018, Clause 3.37)
questions.justification_wrong: Loose cable affects availability; personal use affects confidentiality; identity falsification affects authenticity — only data alteration specifically compromises integrity.

questions.text: A team member denies having sent a specific message. Which aspect of information reliability is at risk?
options: A. Availability
B. Non-repudiation (irrefutability)
C. Integrity
D. Confidentiality
questions.justification_correct: Non-repudiation ensures that the author of an action cannot deny it afterwards — its absence allows senders to deny message transmission. (ISO/IEC 27000:2018, Clause 3.48)
questions.justification_wrong: Availability refers to access; integrity to non-alteration; confidentiality to restricted access — denial of authorship compromises non-repudiation

questions.text: How is the purpose of an information security policy best described?
options: A. Documents risk analysis and the process of seeking countermeasures
B. Provides guidance and management support for information security matters
C. Details technical specifications of each implemented security control
D. Provides comprehensive overview of threats and their possible consequences
questions.justification_correct: The information security policy establishes the organization's direction and commitment to security, providing strategic guidance to management — as per Clause 5.2 of ISO 27001:2022.
questions.justification_wrong: Risk analysis and technical detailing are separate documents; threat mapping is part of risk assessment — the policy provides strategic direction

questions.text: A web server security incident is transferred to a colleague with more technical expertise. What term describes this transfer?
options: A. Functional escalation
B. Hierarchical escalation
C. Operational delegation
D. Responsibility transfer
questions.justification_correct: Functional escalation transfers the incident to someone with specific technical expertise at the same hierarchical level — unlike hierarchical escalation which involves moving up the chain of command.
questions.justification_wrong: Hierarchical escalation involves moving up the chain of command; delegation and transfer are generic terms that do not capture the technical specificity

questions.text: An incident involving unauthorized modification of policy data is recorded in the service center with date, time, description, and possible consequences. Which critical information is MISSING?
options: A. The name of the person who reported the incident
B. The name of the software package used
C. The identification number of the PC involved
D. The list of people who were informed about the incident
questions.justification_correct: The reporter's name is essential information for traceability, accountability, and incident follow-up — without it, it is impossible to contact the source for additional information. (ISO/IEC 27035:2016)
questions.justification_wrong: Software name and PC number are complementary technical data; list of informed parties is produced after recording — the reporter's name is the immediate required data

questions.text: In the incident cycle, which stage occurs immediately after the incident?
options: A. Threat
B. Damage
C. Recovery
D. Prevention
questions.justification_correct: The incident cycle follows the sequence: Threat → Incident → Damage → Recovery. After the incident, damage materializes as a direct consequence. (ISO/IEC 27035:2016)
questions.justification_wrong: Threat precedes the incident; recovery is posterior to damage; prevention acts before the threat — damage is the immediately following stage after the incident

questions.text: Which of the following is a PREVENTIVE security measure?
options: A. Install a logging system that allows determining system changes
B. Shut down all internet traffic after a hacker gains access
C. Store confidential information in a vault with restricted access
D. Activate response team after incident detection
questions.justification_correct: The vault with restricted access prevents unauthorized access before any incident occurs — a preventive measure that eliminates opportunity. (ISO/IEC 27002:2022)
questions.justification_wrong: Logging system is a detective measure; shutting down traffic after access is a repressive/corrective measure; activating team is a reactive measure — none is preventive

questions.text: In case of a fire, which is an example of a REPRESSIVE measure?
options: A. Purchase fire insurance in advance
B. Extinguish the fire after detection by alarm system
C. Repair damage caused by fire after its extinguishing
D. Install smoke detectors in facilities
questions.justification_correct: Extinguishing the fire is a repressive measure — it acts during the incident to contain and minimize ongoing damage. (ISO 22301:2019)
questions.justification_wrong: Insurance and detectors are preventive/detective measures that act before or at the onset of the incident; repairing damage is a corrective measure that acts after the incident

questions.text: What is the primary objective of information classification in an organization?
options: A. Create a manual on how to handle mobile devices
B. Apply labels making information easier to locate
C. Structure information according to its sensitivity and protection requirements
D. Determine the storage cost for each information category
questions.justification_correct: Classification structures information by sensitivity level, determining the appropriate protection controls for each category — per Control A.5.12 of ISO/IEC 27002:2022. (ISO/IEC 27001)
questions.justification_wrong: Mobile devices, labeling for location, and storage costs are distinct objectives; classification aims to determine the required protection level

questions.text: Who is authorized to change the classification of an organizational document?
options: A. The original document author
B. The administrator responsible for storage
C. The owner (proprietor) of the document
D. The department manager where the document was created
questions.justification_correct: The information asset owner is responsible for its classification and authorization of changes to that classification — per Controls A.5.9 and A.5.12 of ISO/IEC 27002:2022. (ISO/IEC 27001)
questions.justification_wrong: Author, administrator, and department manager may have related roles, but the authority to change classification belongs to the proprietor

questions.text: The computer room is protected by an access device reader exclusive to the systems management department. What type of security measure is this?
options: A. Corrective security measure
B. Physical security measure
C. Logical security measure
D. Repressive security measure
questions.justification_correct: Physical access control through a device (card, token) is a physical security measure that protects facilities and hardware against unauthorized access. (ISO/IEC 27002:2022, Control 7.2)
questions.justification_wrong: Corrective and repressive measures act after incidents; logical measures act at software and systems level — the physical access reader is typically a physical control

questions.text: In strong multifactor authentication, which factor is verified when the user presents their access device (card or token)?
options: A. Something you are (biometrics)
B. Something you have (possession)
C. Something you know (knowledge)
D. Something you do (behavior)
questions.justification_correct: The access device represents the possession factor — something the user physically possesses. The three classical factors of strong authentication are: possession, knowledge, and inherence (biometrics). (ISO/IEC)
questions.justification_wrong: Biometrics is the 'something you are' factor; password/PIN is 'something you know'; behavior is a less common additional factor — the physical device represents

questions.text: In physical security with concentric protection zones (protection rings), which of the following is NOT a protection ring?
options: A. The building as perimeter
B. An intermediate ring as a controlled area
C. A specific object or asset as the final target
D. An outer ring as an extended perimeter
questions.justification_correct: In the protection rings approach, the rings are concentric zones (outer, intermediate, inner/building). The 'object' is the asset protected by the set of rings — it is not itself a protection ring
questions.justification_wrong: Building, intermediate ring, and outer ring are legitimate examples of protection rings in the layered security approach.

questions.text: What threat can occur as a result of the absence of an adequate PHYSICAL security measure?
options: A. A user viewing files of another user in the system
B. A server shutting down due to server room overheating
C. A confidential document being left accessible on the printer
D. Hackers freely accessing the computer network remotely
questions.justification_correct: Server overheating results from the absence of physical controls for temperature and air conditioning — a physical protection measure for facility environments. (ISO/IEC 27002:2022, Control 7.5)
questions.justification_wrong: Access to others' files and remote intrusion are logical problems; document on printer is a policy/behavioral issue — overheating is

questions.text: Which of the following is a TECHNICAL (logical) security measure?
options: A. Assigning information to a designated owner
B. Encryption of files and stored data
C. Creating a policy defining what is permitted in emails
D. Storing administrator passwords in a physical safe
questions.justification_correct: Encryption is a technical/logical measure that protects data through mathematical transformation, making it unreadable without the corresponding key. (ISO/IEC 27002:2022, Control 8.24)
questions.justification_wrong: Owner allocation and policy creation are organizational measures; physical safe is a physical measure — only encryption is typically classified

questions.text: Central server backups are kept in the same locked room as the server. What risk does this represent?
options: A. Difficulty in rapid recovery in case of server failure
B. Impossibility of restoration in case of fire that destroys the room
C. Absence of a designated responsible person for backups
D. Easy access by unauthorized persons to the backups
questions.justification_correct: Keeping backups in the same location as the server exposes them to the same physical risks — fire, flooding, or disaster can simultaneously destroy both server and backups, making recovery impossible. (ISO/IEC)
questions.justification_wrong: Recovery speed is not affected by location; responsibility and access control are distinct issues — the critical risk is simultaneous loss

questions.text: What type of malware creates a network of compromised computers controlled remotely?
options: A. Logic bomb
B. Botnet (Storm Worm or similar)
C. Trojan horse
D. Spyware
questions.justification_correct: Botnets are networks of infected devices controlled remotely by an attacker (botmaster), used for DDoS attacks, spam, and other malicious activities. (ISO/IEC 27002:2022, Control 8.7)
questions.justification_wrong: Logic bomb executes actions on specific conditions; Trojan disguises malware as legitimate software; spyware collects information — only botnet creates a network.

questions.text: A workstation was infected via targeted phishing attack. Which action is most effective to PREVENT such incidents in the future?
options: A. Implement Mandatory Access Control (MAC) technology
B. Initiate security awareness and training program
C. Update perimeter firewall rules
D. Update spam filter signatures
questions.justification_correct: Phishing exploits the human factor — awareness is the most effective countermeasure, as it enables employees to recognize and resist social engineering attempts. (ISO/IEC 27002:2022, Control 6.5)
questions.justification_wrong: MAC, firewall, and spam filters are complementary technical controls that do not eliminate the central human vulnerability exploited by phishing.

questions.text: Confidential information has fallen into wrong hands through laptops. What is the FIRST step to propose organizational security measures?
options: A. Formulate comprehensive policy for media and mobile devices
B. Designate information security personnel
C. Encrypt USB drives and laptop hard drives immediately
D. Establish system access control policy
questions.justification_correct: Policy is the starting point — it defines requirements, responsibilities, and necessary controls, guiding all subsequent measures. (ISO/IEC 27002:2022, Control 6.7)
questions.justification_wrong: Designating personnel, encrypting, and access control are specific measures that must be guided by a prior policy — implementing controls without policy foundation is ineffective.

questions.text: What is the name of the system that ensures coherence and systematic management of information security in an organization?
options: A. Information Security Management System (ISMS)
B. Corporate protection rootkit
C. Security regulations for classified government information
D. Intrusion Detection System (IDS)
questions.justification_correct: The ISMS (Information Security Management System) is the set of policies, processes, and controls that ensures systematic and coherent management of information security. (ISO/IEC 27001:2022, Key concept)
questions.justification_wrong: Rootkit is malware; government regulations are specific normative requirements; IDS is a detection tool — only ISMS is the comprehensive management system.

questions.text: What is the process called of verifying whether someone's identity is genuine and matches what was claimed?
options: A. Authentication
B. Authorization
C. Identification
D. Auditing
questions.justification_correct: Authentication is the process of verifying a claimed identity — confirming that the subject is who they claim to be. Identification is merely declaring identity; authorization defines what the authenticated subject may do.
questions.justification_wrong: Identification is declaring who you are; authorization is defining permissions after authentication; auditing is recording and reviewing activities — only authentication verifies identity genuineness.

questions.text: Why is it necessary to maintain an updated disaster recovery plan and test it regularly?
options: A. To always have access to recent backups located outside the office
B. To address operational failures that occur on a daily basis
C. Because in the event of large-scale disruption, outdated measures and procedures may be inadequate
D. Because it is required by personal data protection legislation
questions.justification_correct: Outdated or untested plans can fail precisely when most needed — environments change, personnel leave, systems evolve, making regular testing essential. (ISO 22301:2019, Clause
questions.justification_wrong: Off-site backups and daily failures are partial aspects; personal data protection legislation is not the primary reason for testing recovery plans.

questions.text: Based on which legislation can a person request access to personal data records about them?
options: A. Public records legislation
B. Personal data protection legislation
C. Computer crimes legislation
D. Government information legislation (public access)
questions.justification_correct: Personal data protection legislation (such as LGPD in Brazil or GDPR in Europe) guarantees the data subject's right to access, correct, and request deletion of their data. (LGPD — Law 13.709/2018; GDPR —
questions.justification_wrong: Public records deal with governmental transparency; computer crimes deal with digital offenses; public access deals with government documents

questions.text: Which of the following options represents a legislative or regulatory act that can be imposed on all organizations?
options: A. Intellectual property rights
D. Personal data protection legislation
questions.justification_correct: Personal data protection legislation is mandatory compliance by legal force for all organizations handling personal data. ISO 27001 and 27002 are voluntary certification/best practice standards
questions.justification_wrong: ISO 27001 and 27002 are voluntary standards; intellectual property rights are specific legal protections — only personal data protection legislation

questions.text: Employees use downtime to send personal emails and browse the internet. How can the use of technological resources BEST be regulated from a legal perspective?
options: A. By installing applications that block websites and filter email attachments
B. By developing a code of conduct establishing rights and obligations for both employer and employees
C. By implementing generic privacy regulations
D. By installing antivirus software on all devices
questions.justification_correct: A code of conduct (acceptable use policy) documents the rules of use, rights and obligations for both parties — the strongest legal foundation for regulating technological resource usage. (ISO/IEC 2
questions.justification_wrong: Technical blocks and antivirus are unilateral controls without contractual basis; generic privacy regulations do not specifically address resource use

questions.text: Under what conditions is an employer authorized to verify whether employees use corporate internet and email for private purposes?
options: A. If the employee is informed after each verification is performed
B. If employees are previously aware that such monitoring may occur
C. If a firewall is installed on the corporate network
D. If monitoring is performed only by external auditors
questions.justification_correct: Monitoring is legitimate when employees have been previously informed and have consented — a transparency and consent requirement present in both LGPD and GDPR and labor standards
questions.justification_wrong: Informing after each verification is retroactive and insufficient; firewall and external auditors are not legal conditions for monitoring — prior notification

questions.text: An organization is implementing its ISMS according to ISO 27001:2022 and conducted a risk analysis that identified critical vulnerabilities in its information assets. During the PDCA cycle, in the Planning (Plan) phase, the team must define risk acceptance criteria. Considering that the organization operates in a highly regulated sector (with LGPD requirements) and possesses classified information, which approach is most appropriate?
options: A. Use exclusively quantitative analysis with financial metrics, as ISO 27001:2022 requires numerical proof of all risks before acceptance.
B. Conduct qualitative analysis complemented by quantitative analysis, considering both the regulatory context (LGPD) and the value of information.
C. Apply quantitative analysis only for regulatory compliance risks and qualitative analysis for others, ensuring operational efficiency.
D. Adopt only sectoral benchmarking as an acceptance criterion, comparing the risk profile with other similar organizations.
questions.justification_correct: Risk analysis in ISO 27001:2022 (section 6.1.3) recommends a combination of qualitative and quantitative approaches. For organizations regulated by LGPD and with critical information, qualitative analysis complemented by quantitative analysis provides a comprehensive view that considers both regulatory requirements and actual asset value.
questions.justification_wrong: Option A is incorrect because ISO 27001:2022 does not require exclusively quantitative analysis; it recognizes both qualitative and quantitative methods as valid.

questions.text: An organization implemented an ISMS according to ISO 27001:2022 and is conducting its first information security risk analysis. During the process, the team identified that information classification was incomplete, affecting the accuracy of the assessment. When implementing a qualitative risk analysis approach in this scenario, what is the primary limitation that the information security manager should be aware of?
options: A. Qualitative analysis depends on subjective judgments and perceptions of evaluators, reducing objectivity and comparability between different assessments.
B. Qualitative analysis always requires a second opinion from external auditors certified by PECB to validate the results.
C. Qualitative analysis cannot be used when unclassified information exists, and one must wait for complete classification before proceeding.
D. Qualitative analysis automatically eliminates all low-probability risks, making it unsuitable for organizations with sensitive information.
questions.justification_correct: According to ISO 27001:2022, section A.12.6.1, and risk analysis guidelines in A.5.2, the qualitative approach uses scales (high, medium, low) or risk matrices that depend heavily on the subjective assessment of evaluators, which can reduce objectivity and the ability to compare assessments across different time periods or teams.
questions.justification_wrong: B is incorrect because ISO 27001:2022 does not require external validation of internal qualitative analyses; it recommends documentation and periodic review, not mandatory external audits.

questions.text: An organization implemented its ISMS framework according to ISO 27001:2022 and conducted a risk analysis using qualitative methodology. During the risk treatment process, the team identified that some risks were evaluated with 'Medium' probability and 'High' impact. Considering the PDCA cycle and risk management principles, what should be the priority approach for these risks?
options: A. Accept all 'Medium-High' risks because they do not fall into the critical risk category, documenting only the decision.
B. Implement controls to reduce probability and/or impact, submitting them for senior management approval and monitoring their effectiveness in the Check phase.
C. Automatically transfer all risks to specialized insurance companies, eliminating the need for internal technical controls.
D. Ignore the probability and implement controls based only on 'High' impact, without considering the established risk matrix.
questions.justification_correct: According to ISO 27001:2022, clause 6.1.3, information security risks must be treated through selection and implementation of appropriate controls. Risks with medium probability and high impact represent significant risk levels that warrant active treatment through control implementation, with oversight from senior management and continuous monitoring.
questions.justification_wrong: Option A violates the risk management principle by accepting significant risks without adequate treatment. Option C represents a risk transfer approach that does not eliminate the need for internal controls; Option D ignores the risk matrix methodology.

questions.text: An organization is implementing its ISMS according to ISO 27001:2022 and needs to define criteria for information classification. During the analysis, it identifies that information of high strategic value, with a long legal retention period, and that would cause significant business harm if disclosed, should receive differentiated protection levels. Which approach BEST integrates these criteria?
options: A. Establish a multidimensional classification scheme based on impact factors of confidentiality, integrity, availability, and retention period.
B. Automatically apply the maximum protection level to all information with retention periods exceeding two years, since retention time is the primary factor.
C. Use only the direct financial impact criterion to determine classification, since strategic value is a subjective concept and difficult to measure.
D. Delegate classification exclusively to each information owner, waiving objective criteria, since each stakeholder better understands their information.
questions.justification_correct: Alternative A is correct as it adequately reflects the approach prescribed by ISO 27001:2022, especially in clauses 5.15 (Identification of information requirements) and 6.1.3 (Risk evaluation), which require a comprehensive approach considering multiple factors including confidentiality, integrity, availability, and retention requirements.
questions.justification_wrong: B is incorrect because it overvalues retention period as the sole classification factor without considering actual impact as required by ISO 27001:2022; C is too narrow in focus; D lacks the objective criteria necessary for consistent and auditable classification decisions.

questions.text: An organization implemented an ISMS according to ISO 27001:2022 and is conducting information security risk analysis. During this process, it identified that critical information uses simple authentication (password only) for access, while other less critical information has multi-factor authentication. When evaluating the vulnerability associated with the absence of multi-factor authentication for critical information, this should be classified as:
options: A. A technical vulnerability that reduces the ability to detect unauthorized access, and should be evaluated considering the probability of exploitation and potential impact.
B. A human threat that occurs exclusively when employees conduct deliberate internal attacks, requiring only disciplinary measures.
C. An information classification failure that should be corrected by reclassifying the information as non-critical, eliminating the need for stronger controls.
D. An acceptable risk that does not require treatment, as simple authentication meets the non-repudiation requirements mandated by ISO 27001:2022.
questions.justification_correct: The correct answer is A. According to ISO 27001:2022, clause 6.1, the organization must determine information security risks through systematic analysis. A vulnerability is a weakness in the security posture that can be exploited; the absence of multi-factor authentication for critical information is a technical vulnerability that increases the risk of unauthorized access and should be evaluated within the risk assessment framework.
questions.justification_wrong: B is incorrect because vulnerabilities are not exclusively human threats; the absence of multi-factor authentication is a technical weakness that can be exploited by various threat actors. C is incorrect because it proposes reclassification rather than addressing the control gap. D is incorrect because simple authentication does not meet non-repudiation requirements and represents an unacceptable risk for critical information.

questions.text: During the implementation of an ISMS in accordance with ISO 27001:2022, an organization conducts risk analysis identifying multiple vulnerabilities in its information assets. When considering a specific weak authentication vulnerability in critical systems, the security team must decide between applying a preventive control measure or a detective measure. Which of the following options is best
questions.tag: Preventive vs Detective Controls and Hierarchy of Measures
options: A. Implement multi-factor authentication (MFA) before any unauthorized access occurs, since preventive measures reduce the probability
B. Implement detailed access logs and alerts for failed authentication attempts, since detective measures are sufficient to eliminate risk
C. Wait for a security incident to occur before implementing corrective measures, allowing validation of the real materialization of the risk before
D. Apply only repressive measures such as account blocking after fraud detection, dispensing with preventive or detective controls on initial access
questions.justification_correct: The correct answer is A. According to ISO 27001:2022, section 6.1 (Information security risk assessment), the organization must determine and evaluate risks. The hierarchy of controls prioritizes preventive measure
questions.justification_wrong: Option B is incorrect because detective measures, although important, only identify problems that have already occurred and do not eliminate risks; they must be co

questions.text: An organization is reviewing its information security risk analysis process. During qualitative assessment, the team identifies a critical vulnerability in a legacy system with no immediate possibility of remediation. The risk was classified as 'Extreme' with potential impact of 8 hours of critical service unavailability. Which approach MUST the organization adopt in accordance with
questions.tag: Risk Treatment and Acceptance in ISMS
options: A. Implement compensating controls (detective/corrective measures) to mitigate the risk while working on the permanent technical solution, document
B. Formally accept the risk without implementing additional controls, since the remediation is not economically viable in the short term
C. Completely transfer the risk through cyber security insurance contract, eliminating organizational responsibility
D. Avoid the risk by discontinuing the legacy system immediately, regardless of the operational impact on the organization's activities
questions.justification_correct: According to ISO 27001:2022, clause 6.1.3 (Risk Treatment Process), when a risk cannot be eliminated or corrected immediately, the organization MUST implement compensating controls
questions.justification_wrong: Alternative B is incorrect because risk acceptance without compensating controls violates the fundamental principle of ISMS - reduce risks to acceptable levels

questions.text: An organization has implemented an ISMS in accordance with ISO 27001:2022 and is refining its information security risk analysis strategy. During qualitative assessment, the team identified that the absence of an adequate incident response plan represents a critical gap. What is the most significant implication of this gap when considering the framework of security measures (preven
options: A. The organization will lose the capability to implement preventive measures, making it impossible to prevent future threats to the technology environment.
B. The incident detection time will increase significantly, compromising the effectiveness of detective measures and limiting the implementation of opportun
C. Qualitative risk analysis will become unfeasible, requiring exclusive adoption of quantitative methodology for control validation.
D. The implementation of multi-factor authentication will be considered ineffective, requiring replacement by solutions based on digital certificates.
questions.justification_correct: According to ISO 27001:2022 (section 8.3 - Risk assessment) and ISO 27035:2016 (Incident management), the absence of an incident response plan particularly compromises the capability to detect and r
questions.justification_wrong: Option A is incorrect because preventive measures are independent of incident response and can be implemented in isolation (e.g., access policies

questions.text: An organization has implemented an ISMS in accordance with ISO 27001:2022 and identified that multiple technical vulnerabilities in its web applications allow unauthorized access to customer personal data. During qualitative risk analysis, the team classified this scenario as high risk. When considering the PDCA cycle and protection measures, which sequence of information security actions BEST
options: A. Implement penetration testing (detective measure), fix vulnerabilities in code (preventive measure), apply WAF (preventive measure) and reg
B. Immediately isolate affected servers (repressive measure), disable access to web applications (corrective measure), communicate incident to org
C. Increase monitoring of suspicious employees (detective measure), implement multi-factor authentication (preventive measure), conduct quantitative analysis
D. Hire external consultancy immediately (corrective measure), implement information classification (preventive measure), review protection zones
questions.justification_correct: Answer A presents the correct sequence of measures according to ISO 27001:2022 and the PDCA cycle. Penetration testing is a detective measure that identifies vulnerabilities. Vulnerability remediation is
questions.justification_wrong: B inappropriately mixes repressive measures (late isolation) with corrective measures (disabling) before adequate preventive measures, not following the PD

questions.text: During the critical review of an Information Security Management System (ISMS) in accordance with ISO 27001:2022, top management identified that the effectiveness metrics of controls are not being collected adequately. The organization wishes to establish a robust process for collecting and analyzing performance data that enables evidence-based decision making and continuous improvement of the SGS
questions.tag: Monitoring and Measurement of ISMS Performance
options: A. Implement key performance indicators (KPIs) based exclusively on regulatory compliance and external audit, with annual review of the al
B. Establish quantitative and qualitative metrics that evaluate the effectiveness of controls, including incident detection rate, response time
C. Delegate performance data collection to the IT department, without need for top management involvement, focusing only on technical metrics
D. Perform measurements only when security incidents occur, using this information for reactive adjustments to the ISMS
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022, clause 9.1, requires that the organization establish what needs to be monitored and measured, the methods of monitoring and measurement, when results will be analyzed and evaluated, and wh
questions.justification_wrong: Alternative A is limited by focusing only on regulatory compliance, neglecting the continuous evaluation of control effectiveness. Alternative C vi
